extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won a flat sprint in Nîmes after MOHORIČ's leadout accidentally positioned him third wheel behind RICHEZE-VIVIANI, allowing him to time his sprint perfectly.
tactical_patterns: MOHORIČ's leadout for VIVIANI inadvertently gave EWAN third wheel inside final 200m, proving third wheel trumps designated train.
Modern peloton team clustering caused Astana-Sunweb collision 16km from finish, demonstrating positional risk when squads travel compactly.
FUGLSANG abandoned while holding 8th GC after second crash, suggesting cumulative damage from stage 1 crash compromised handling.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: Perfect sprint timing from third wheel shows he can win without organized leadout in chaotic finishes.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: Early crash clipping curb with locked wheels suggests handling issue, but returned quickly without apparent injury.
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: negative: Abandoned 16km from finish while 8th GC after holding injured left hand, likely cumulative damage from stage 1.
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Had perfect RICHEZE leadout third wheel but couldn't close sprint, suggests power deficit at this Tour.
rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: neutral: Led out VIVIANI effectively but inadvertently gave EWAN winning position, showing leadout discipline but tactical oversight.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: Fourth place finish adds points despite chaotic sprint, maintaining green jersey position without winning.
